name: Isoka, Angel of Serpents
description:
Isoka, Angel of Serpents

*{Lore Isoka, sister to Ikasha (Shadow) and Inaea (Mercy), completes the Triad of Transformation. Where thresholds and compassion mark her sisters' domains, Isoka teaches that every self is temporary --- identity is a skin to be shed so growth can continue. Her serpents are omens and teachers: each cast skin a lesson in release; each venom a catalyst for necessary change. Those who walk her path become alchemists of the self, embracing dissolution as the first motion of rebirth.

Her followers range from quiet hedgewitches who use venom to heal and shed skins to ease grief, to the darker cults of the Coiled Heart, where initiates drink serpent venom to burn away their old selves and emerge as something new --- or something monstrous. Isoka does not judge; she offers the knife and the poison both. The choice is the seeker's.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Isoka is called by many names. In Zakov, she is the Tide-Shedder, to whom pirates leave a lock of hair and a name at her sunken temple; eels eat both. In Acasia, she is the Copperhead, a hedge-cult that sheds identities to infiltrate noble houses. In the Valewood, her witches are called Scale-Mothers, who tend the boundary between the living and the shedding dead. Wherever change is feared or embraced, Isoka watches.

quote "Do not mourn the skin you shed. It was never meant to last. The venom that burns away the old self is the same that grants the strength to become new." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Serpentine Transformation: shedding identity, renewal through dissolution Venom as Catalyst: poison that heals or destroys, the paradox of change Shedding the Past: erasing debts, memories, and old allegiances Healthy Change to Monstrous Rebirth: from gentle alchemy to sword-and-sorcery cults itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Isoka does not bind a familiar of permanence. Their Thiasos is a small creature of shedding and renewal: a snake that sheds its skin weekly, a gecko that regrows its tail, a salamander that thrives in the ashes of a burned identity, a small adder that sheds its venom with each bite, or a blind worm that lives beneath the earth and emerges only to transform. This creature requires no food in the usual sense, but it must be allowed to shed something (skin, a tooth, a claw) each week. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it grows dull and sluggish, and its keeper suffers --1 die on rolls to resist unwanted transformation or change until it next sheds).

The Codex of Isoka is never a book of fixed ink. It may be a bundle of shed snakeskins, each inscribed with a single Rite that fades as the skin dries; a slate that must be washed clean after each reading; a scroll of living parchment that flakes away as the Rite is learned; or a set of obsidian tablets that must be shattered and recarved after each use. Because Isoka's truths are written in the act of shedding, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week re-inscribing the Rites from memory onto fresh material (new skin, new slate), or the patterns become illegible (treat as Compromised until re-inscribed).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Isoka may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a word of shedding. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Escape or Deception rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer would be bound, grappled, or restrained, they may break free as a free action (shedding the constraint like a snake sheds its skin). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Isoka.
summary: Isoka, Angel of Serpents *{Lore Isoka, sister to Ikasha (Shadow) and Inaea (Mercy), completes the Triad of Transformation. Where thresholds and compassion mark her sisters' domains, Isoka teaches that every self is temporary --- identity is a skin to be shed so growth can continue.

rites.name: Rite of the Loosening Skin
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A discarded snakeskin or a strand of your own hair. Effect: Shrug off a temporary burden. Remove one Condition (Fear, Poison, Fatigue, or a minor social stain) that you have acquired this scene. Alternatively, reroll one failed roll to escape restraint or pursuit. Push It: Also ignore one environmental penalty (difficult terrain, smoke, etc.) for the scene, but leave a visible trace of your passage. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Shrug off a temporary burden. Remove one Condition (Fear, Poison, Fatigue, or a minor social stain) that you have acquired this scene. Alternatively, reroll one failed roll to escape restraint or pursuit. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Forked Tongue
rites.description: (Basic, 6 XP) Scene; Self; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A harmless lie whispered to a mirror. Effect: For one scene, you may speak ambiguously without penalty. When you use Deception or Sway to conceal your true intent or pass as someone else, you may treat a Partial Success as a Clean Success (the lie holds). On a Miss, however, the GM gains 2 SB as your deceit unravels in an embarrassing way. Push It: Extend the effect to one willing ally; they may also lie freely for the scene. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For one scene, you may speak ambiguously without penalty. When you use Deception or Sway to conceal your true intent or pass as someone else, you may treat a Partial Success as a Clean Success (the lie holds). On a Miss, however, the GM gains 2 SB as your deceit unravels in an embarrassing way. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Shedded Self
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A complete change of clothing and a token from your former role (a badge, a lock of hair, a written name). Effect: You shed one persistent identity --- a title, a bond, a debt, or a reputation. For the remainder of the scene, you are treated as a stranger to those who knew you in that role. You gain +2 dice on any roll to avoid detection, escape pursuit, or convince others that you are not yourself. Create a 6-segment Shed Identity timer; when it fills, the old identity returns as a separate NPC (a rival, a witness, or a ghost). Push It: Immediately erase one minor obligation (a Debt Timer segment or 1 Obligation to a Patron) as you slough it off. Mark 1 SB (Clubs) as the discarded debt finds a new debtor. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You shed one persistent identity --- a title, a bond, a debt, or a reputation. For the remainder of the scene, you are treated as a stranger to those who knew you in that role. You gain +2 dice on any roll to avoid detection, escape pursuit, or convince others that you are not yourself. Create a 6-segment Shed Identity timer; when it fills, the old identity returns as a separate NPC (a rival, a witness, or a ghost). \\

rites.name: Rite of Venom's Paradox
rites.description: (Advanced, 10 XP) Instant; Touch; No. Tags: , , Materials: A drop of venom from a serpent that has shed its skin within the last day. Effect: Inflict or cure through poison's paradox. Choose one: itemize Cursed Venom: Touch a creature and inject venom. They suffer +1 Harm (poison) and must test Resolve (DV 4) or gain the Poisoned Condition (-1 die to physical actions until cured). Mercy's Antidote: Touch a poisoned or diseased creature. The venom neutralizes the malady; remove one Poisoned or Diseased Condition, and the target clears 1 Fatigue. itemize Push It: Apply both effects to the same target (curse and cleanse in a painful purge, representing a violent rebirth), or affect two different targets. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Inflict or cure through poison's paradox. Choose one: itemize Cursed Venom: Touch a creature and inject venom. They suffer +1 Harm (poison) and must test Resolve (DV 4) or gain the Poisoned Condition (-1 die to physical actions until cured). Mercy's Antidote: Touch a poisoned or diseased creature. The venom neutralizes the malady; remove one Poisoned or Diseased Condition, and the target clears 1 Fatigue. itemize

rites.name: Rite of Unremembered Metamorphosis
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Self; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A full identity kit, a serpent's shed skin, and a personal item from the life you are leaving (or records of your old life to burn). Effect: Choose one: itemize Complete Metamorphosis: After an hour of ritual, you emerge with a new face, voice, and documented identity. For one week, you are immune to recognition by anyone who knew your previous self, and gain +2 dice on all Deception and Stealth rolls related to that new identity. Create an 8-segment New Identity timer; when it fills, the old self may reassert itself (GM's discretion). Cast-Off History: After a full night's ritual burning all mundane records of your old life, your previous identity becomes unreliable. Common records and casual memories become vague; those who track you via that identity suffer --2 dice. You gain +2 dice on rolls relying on your new identity. Create a 6-segment Erased Past timer; when it fills, one intimate from your old life senses the deception and may investigate. itemize Push It: Extend the metamorphosis to a willing ally, make the change permanent (lose access to one Bond or Minor Asset), or arrange a plausible "death" for your old self (a funeral, a witness). Mark +2 Obligation and the GM gains 2 SB (the world strains to remember what was lost). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: 1 week.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Complete Metamorphosis: After an hour of ritual, you emerge with a new face, voice, and documented identity. For one week, you are immune to recognition by anyone who knew your previous self, and gain +2 dice on all Deception and Stealth rolls related to that new identity. Create an 8-segment New Identity timer; when it fills, the old self may reassert itself (GM's discretion). Cast-Off History: After a full night's ritual burning all mundane records of your old life, your previous identity becomes unreliable. Common records and casual memories become vague; those who track you via that identity suffer --2 dice. You gain +2 dice on rolls relying on your new identity. Create a 6-segment Erased Past timer; when it fills, one intimate from your old life senses the deception and may investigate. itemize
